message:
fix: kosaraju_strongly_connected_components order matches nx (br-r37-c1-53cwv)

The Rust _raw_kosaraju_strongly_connected_components emitted SCCs
in reverse order from nx's Kosaraju-completion contract. For
example on a DAG, fnx yielded [{a},{b},{c},{d},{e}] while nx
yields [{e},{d},{c},{b},{a}]. On a multi-SCC DiGraph with a
bridge, fnx yielded [{a,b},{c,d}] while nx yields [{c,d},{a,b}].

Drop-in code that iterated SCCs in nx's Kosaraju order broke.

Replace the local materialise + reorder-on-source flow with a
direct delegation to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity, threading
the source= kwarg through. Preserve the standard
@not_implemented_for(undirected) error message (br-r37-c1-4elbw)
via a pre-check before delegation. Mirrors the sister fix
br-r37-c1-2vdtt for strongly_connected_components.

9 parity tests in test_kosaraju_scc_order_parity.py freeze the
contract: DAG, two-SCC-with-bridge, single-cycle, disjoint-two-
cycles, int-node DAG, source= kwarg, undirected error,
empty digraph, and isolated-nodes (each own SCC).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -12,6 +12,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3lqi6","title":"DiGraph.reverse() / fnx.reverse() edge + adj iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.reverse() (and DiGraph.reverse() method) on a DiGraph returns a copy whose edge-iteration order and per-node adj-iteration order differ from nx.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','d')]  # path
  fnx.reverse(dg).edges() -> [('c','b'),('d','c'),('b','a')]
  nx.reverse(dgx).edges() -> [('b','a'),('c','b'),('d','c')]

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]  # cycle
  fnx -> [(1,0),(3,2),(0,3),(2,1)]
  nx  -> [(0,3),(1,0),(2,1),(3,2)]

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','a'),('c','a')]
  fnx adj['a'] = ['d', 'c']
  nx adj['a']  = ['c', 'd']

nx iterates nodes in insertion order and emits reversed (v→u) edges in that order; fnx's Rust-side reverse traverses internal adjacency in a different order.

The copy=False path matches. The copy=True path (default for both function and method) drifts.

Fix: delegate the copy=True path to nx so the resulting DiGraph's edge + adj iteration matches nx's contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:56:39.907591704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:59:41.487970050Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:59:41.485884732Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-4d97w","title":"_from_nx_graph (les_miserables / Davis / hoffman_singleton) drifts adj order from nx","description":"fnx's _from_nx_graph converter walks nx.G.edges() in nx's edge-iteration order and feeds them to add_edges_from. Because nx.G.edges() canonicalises each edge to '(first-discovered, second-discovered)' direction, fnx's resulting graph has adj order following the canonical edge traversal — NOT matching the nx graph's per-node adj insertion order.

This is the inverse of br-r37-c1-sgnab (_fnx_to_nx adj order), which we fixed using a per-node-queue topological emit. Same fix applies here in reverse direction.

Repro (les_miserables_graph delegates via _from_nx_graph):
  nx.les_miserables_graph().adj['Valjean']:
    ['Labarre','MmeMagloire','MlleBaptistine','Myriel','Marguerite','MmeDeR']
  fnx.les_miserables_graph().adj['Valjean']:
    ['Myriel','MlleBaptistine','MmeMagloire','Labarre','Marguerite','MmeDeR']

Affects every nx-to-fnx delegated graph: les_miserables_graph, davis_southern_women_graph, hoffman_singleton_graph (per their docstrings), plus algorithms that use _from_nx_graph (e.g. union, intersection, transitive_reduction, transitive_closure_dag, etc.).

Fix: route _from_nx_graph through the existing _topo_emit_edges_by_adj helper, which already does per-node-queue topological emission to preserve adj order across graph-format conversions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:11:40.761421100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:17:23.441351537Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:17:23.439301764Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-4elbw","title":"parity: 4 more functions raise NotImplemented/NetworkXError with custom messages instead of nx's standard wording","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-yf4tf — 4 more functions diverge from nx's exact error messages: (1) condensation on undirected raises 'strongly_connected_components is not defined for undirected graphs. Use connected_components instead.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (2) flow_hierarchy on undirected raises NetworkXError('flow_hierarchy requires a DiGraph'); nx raises NetworkXError('G must be a digraph in flow_hierarchy'). (3) in_degree_centrality and (4) out_degree_centrality on undirected raise custom 'is not defined for undirected graphs.' messages; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. Drop-in code matching the exact text breaks. Fix: align all four to nx's exact wording.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:28:50.121233008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.902270771Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.900331589Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 35bb5d3. strongly_connected_components/number_strongly/kosaraju/condensation/in_degree_centrality/out_degree_centrality/flow_hierarchy now raise with nx's exact error messages. 17 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-53cwv","title":"kosaraju_strongly_connected_components emission order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components emits SCCs in reverse order vs nx (similar to br-r37-c1-2vdtt for strongly_connected_components, but for the Kosaraju variant).

Repro:
  edges = [(a,b),(a,c),(b,d),(c,d),(d,e)] (DAG)
  fnx -> [{a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, {e}]
  nx  -> [{e}, {d}, {c}, {b}, {a}]

  edges = [(a,b),(b,a),(c,d),(d,c),(a,c)] (two SCCs + bridge)
  fnx -> [{a,b}, {c,d}]
  nx  -> [{c,d}, {a,b}]

Drop-in code that iterates SCCs in nx's Kosaraju-completion order broke. Fix: delegate kosaraju_strongly_connected_components to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors the existing strongly_connected_components fix).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:24:32.967954855Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:25:21.708346574Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-5jbv9","title":"minimum_node_cut / minimum_edge_cut return different valid cuts than nx","description":"fnx.minimum_node_cut and fnx.minimum_edge_cut return cuts of correct minimum size, but pick a different (equally valid) cut than nx on graphs where multiple cuts of the same size exist. The choice depends on internal adj-iteration / DFS-augmenting-path traversal.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]
  fnx.minimum_node_cut(g) -> {'c'}
  nx.minimum_node_cut(gx)  -> {'d'}

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)] (path)
  fnx.minimum_node_cut(g) -> {1}
  nx.minimum_node_cut(gx)  -> {3}

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]
  fnx.minimum_edge_cut(g) -> {('d','e')}
  nx.minimum_edge_cut(gx)  -> {('d','c')}

Both implementations produce a correct minimum cut, but drop-in code that compared the cut against a reference set broke.

Fix: delegate minimum_node_cut and minimum_edge_cut to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors the cycle_basis/find_cycle/strongly_connected_components/max_weight_matching pattern).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:15:19.716715766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:18:44.580433219Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:18:44.578270474Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-5nop9","title":"parity: bfs_labeled_edges public signature should be (G, sources) matching networkx","description":"fnx.bfs_labeled_edges has signature (G, sources=None, sort_neighbors=None, *, source=None) — three problems vs nx (G, sources): sources is required in nx but optional here, sort_neighbors is not part of upstream signature, source is a keyword-only legacy alias. Even though both shapes appear to function, the public signature divergence is observable via inspect.signature, breaks parameter introspection used by typed wrappers, and means typer-style strict argument routing breaks. Tighten the wrapper to (G, sources) matching nx; if downstream callers pass sort_neighbors/source those are unsupported by nx and should fail loudly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:09:39.386409859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:16:47.912406594Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:16:47.910554548Z","close_reason":"Shipped in bf5195e: bfs_labeled_edges sig tightened to (G, sources). Removed sort_neighbors and source= legacy params. 3 existing tests still pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-5npb6","title":"parity: Graph class method signatures hidden by @wraps decorator on top of Rust-bound methods","description":"Many Graph/DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph methods are wrapped via @wraps(rust_impl) — the Rust impl has signature () so functools.wraps overwrites the Python wrapper's documented signature with the catch-all. inspect.signature(g.to_directed) returns () instead of (self, as_view=False). 53 method-signature drifts surface across the four Graph classes: add_edge (u_of_edge, v_of_edge), add_node (node_for_adding), add_weighted_edges_from (ebunch_to_add), remove_node (n vs node), remove_nodes_from (nodes vs bunch), remove_edges_from (ebunch vs bunch), edges/nodes/get_edge_data (args/kwargs vs documented surface), to_directed/to_undirected (missing as_view kwarg in introspection). Fix: replace @wraps(rust_impl) with @wraps(nx_method) where appropriate, OR drop wraps and manually set __doc__/__name__, OR delete __wrapped__ on the wrapper. Add parity tests asserting inspect.signature(method) matches nx for all four classes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:14:17.351791844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:17:19.848195304Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:17:19.846339454Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 2026bc3. Dropped @wraps(rust_impl) from to_directed/to_undirected wrappers (rust impls have empty () signatures that poisoned inspect.signature via __wrapped__). Now inspect.signature(g.to_directed) returns nx's (as_view=False) and DiGraph.to_undirected returns (reciprocal=False, as_view=False). 11 parity tests freeze the contract. Note: this is a partial fix for the broader 53-method drift sweep — remaining drifts (add_edge/add_node parameter names, edges/nodes/get_edge_data catch-all from PyO3 bindings) need separate beads since they require Rust binding changes or per-method Python wrappers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -9900,32 +9900,22 @@ def biconnected_component_edges(G):
 def kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(G, source=None):
     """Kosaraju-style strongly connected components.
 
-    Each component is returned as a ``set`` to match networkx; the Rust
-    helper yields lists. ``source`` is accepted for networkx signature
-    parity — when provided, the component containing ``source`` is
-    yielded first, followed by the remaining SCCs in the Rust emission
-    order.
+    Each component is returned as a ``set`` to match networkx.
+
+    br-r37-c1-53cwv: the Rust helper emitted SCCs in reverse order
+    from nx's Kosaraju-completion contract (e.g. on a DAG, fnx
+    yielded ``[{a},{b},{c},{d},{e}]`` while nx yields
+    ``[{e},{d},{c},{b},{a}]``). Delegate to nx so emission order
+    matches its documented Kosaraju traversal exactly. Mirrors the
+    sister strongly_connected_components fix (br-r37-c1-2vdtt).
     """
-    try:
-        sccs = [set(component) for component in _raw_kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(G)]
-    except NetworkXNotImplemented as exc:
-        # br-r37-c1-4elbw: align with nx's standard message.
-        if "not defined for undirected" in str(exc):
-            raise NetworkXNotImplemented(
-                "not implemented for undirected type"
-            ) from exc
-        raise
-    if source is None:
-        yield from sccs
-        return
-    for component in sccs:
-        if source in component:
-            yield component
-            break
-    for component in sccs:
-        if source in component:
-            continue
-        yield component
+    if not G.is_directed():
+        # Preserve the standard '@not_implemented_for' error format
+        # so callers see a stable message (br-r37-c1-4elbw).
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for undirected type")
+    yield from _call_networkx_for_parity(
+        "kosaraju_strongly_connected_components", G, source=source,
+    )
 
 
 def node_connected_component(G, n):

@@ -0,0 +1,120 @@
+"""Parity for ``kosaraju_strongly_connected_components`` emission order.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-53cwv. The Rust helper emitted SCCs in reverse order
+from nx's Kosaraju-completion contract. For example on a DAG, fnx
+yielded ``[{a},{b},{c},{d},{e}]`` while nx yields
+``[{e},{d},{c},{b},{a}]``. Drop-in code that iterated SCCs in nx's
+order broke.
+
+Mirrors the sister fix br-r37-c1-2vdtt for
+``strongly_connected_components``.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+def _make_dg(lib, edges):
+    g = lib.DiGraph()
+    for u, v in edges:
+        g.add_edge(u, v)
+    return g
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_dag_scc_order_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("a", "b"), ("a", "c"), ("b", "d"), ("c", "d"), ("d", "e")]
+    dg = _make_dg(fnx, edges)
+    dgx = _make_dg(nx, edges)
+    assert list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dg)) == list(
+        nx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dgx)
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_two_scc_with_bridge_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("a", "b"), ("b", "a"), ("c", "d"), ("d", "c"), ("a", "c")]
+    dg = _make_dg(fnx, edges)
+    dgx = _make_dg(nx, edges)
+    assert list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dg)) == list(
+        nx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dgx)
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_single_cycle_one_scc_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("c", "a")]
+    dg = _make_dg(fnx, edges)
+    dgx = _make_dg(nx, edges)
+    assert list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dg)) == list(
+        nx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dgx)
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_disjoint_two_cycles_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("a", "b"), ("b", "a"), ("c", "d"), ("d", "c")]
+    dg = _make_dg(fnx, edges)
+    dgx = _make_dg(nx, edges)
+    assert list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dg)) == list(
+        nx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dgx)
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_int_node_dag_matches_nx():
+    edges = [(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)]
+    dg = _make_dg(fnx, edges)
+    dgx = _make_dg(nx, edges)
+    assert list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dg)) == list(
+        nx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dgx)
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_with_source_kwarg_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("a", "b"), ("b", "a"), ("c", "d"), ("d", "c"), ("a", "c")]
+    dg = _make_dg(fnx, edges)
+    dgx = _make_dg(nx, edges)
+    assert list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dg, source="a")) == list(
+        nx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dgx, source="a")
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_undirected_input_raises_not_implemented():
+    g = fnx.Graph([(0, 1)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXNotImplemented, match="undirected"):
+        list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(g))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_empty_digraph_matches_nx():
+    dg = fnx.DiGraph()
+    dgx = nx.DiGraph()
+    assert list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dg)) == list(
+        nx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dgx)
+    ) == []
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_isolated_nodes_each_own_scc_matches_nx():
+    dg = fnx.DiGraph()
+    dgx = nx.DiGraph()
+    for n in ["a", "b", "c"]:
+        dg.add_node(n)
+        dgx.add_node(n)
+    assert list(fnx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dg)) == list(
+        nx.kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(dgx)
+    )
